title: Sticky Lemon-Honey Chicken & Super Crunch Potatoes
description: Crispy, sticky chicken leg quarters roasted on a wire rack, paired with extra crunchy potatoes and buttery pan-fried mushrooms.
steps: Mix all the marinade ingredients (3 tbsp olive oil, lemon juice, honey, soy sauce, garlic, Dijon, paprika, herbs, salt, and pepper) in a large bowl or Ziplock bag.
Add the chicken legs, massage well to coat, and let them marinate at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, par-boil the potato chunks in salted water for 8–10 minutes until just knife-tender. Drain, let steam dry for 1 minute, and shake vigorously in the colander to rough up the edges.
Toss the roughed-up potatoes with 3 tbsp of olive oil and a generous pinch of salt.
Preheat your oven to 200°C. Line your baking pan with foil or parchment and place the wire rack on top.
Remove the chicken from the bag, saving the leftover marinade in a small bowl. Place the chicken on the wire rack.
Spread the potatoes evenly on the dark metal tray.
Place both the chicken tray and the potato tray into the oven and roast for 45–50 minutes.
At the 30-minute mark, open the oven to brush the chicken with the saved marinade and give the potatoes a shake to flip them.
During the final 5–10 minutes of baking, heat 1 tbsp of butter in a frying pan over medium-high heat and sauté the sliced mushrooms until golden brown.
Remove the trays from the oven. Toss the hot, crunchy potatoes with 1 tbsp of butter.
Serve the sticky chicken topped with the buttery pan-fried mushrooms alongside the potatoes.
